model_narrative: This is a Resource-Task-Network scheduling problem for a small batch chemical plant. Across a discrete time horizon the plant decides, at each time point, which processing tasks to trigger and how large a batch each triggered task should run. Tracking the running inventory of every resource over time is part of the bookkeeping. The goal is to meet the external material schedule while triggering as few task executions as possible, so the objective minimizes the total number of task triggers over the horizon.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: processing tasks (reactions)

sets.name: R
sets.doc: resources: reactants, products, and the reactor-availability resource V

sets.name: T
sets.doc: time points; point 0 only carries the initial inventory

sets.name: T1
sets.doc: decision time points, excluding the initialization point 0

sets.name: Ir
sets.members: Ir[r] is the set of tasks that touch resource r
sets.doc: for each resource, the tasks connected to it in the network

params.name: mu
params.doc: fixed consumption or production of a resource by a task at offset theta time points after the task is triggered; sign convention: negative is consumption, positive is production; offset 0 is the trigger instant, offset tau is reaction completion

params.name: nu
params.doc: batch-size-proportional consumption or production of a resource by a task at offset theta after trigger; same sign convention as mu; for reactants this is the per-unit draw at trigger, for products the per-unit yield at completion

params.name: tau
params.doc: processing duration of each task in time points

params.name: Vmin
params.doc: minimum batch size when a task is run

params.name: Vmax
params.doc: maximum batch size when a task is run

params.name: X0
params.doc: initial inventory of each resource at time point 0

params.name: Xmin
params.doc: minimum allowed inventory (safety stock) of each resource

params.name: Xmax
params.doc: maximum allowed inventory (storage capacity) of each resource

params.name: Pi
params.doc: scheduled external inflow or outflow of a resource at each time point; positive is delivery in, negative is demand out

vars.name: X
vars.doc: inventory level of each resource at each time point; X[r,0] is fixed to the initial inventory

vars.name: N
vars.doc: 1 if task i is triggered at time point t, else 0

vars.name: E
vars.doc: batch size of task i triggered at time point t; zero if not triggered

objective.expr_var: total number of task triggers over the horizon

description: For every resource at every decision time point, the inventory carried at that point must equal the inventory carried at the previous point, adjusted by the net effect of all tasks acting on that resource and by the scheduled external inflow or outflow for that resource at that point. A task contributes to a resource according to how long ago it was triggered, combining a fixed per-trigger effect with an effect proportional to its batch size, and only contributions from triggers that have already occurred within the task duration count.

description: For every resource at every decision time point, the inventory carried at that point must stay at or above the minimum allowed level for that resource.

description: For every task at every decision time point, if the task is triggered then its batch size must be at least the minimum batch size for that task, and if it is not triggered its batch size is forced to zero.

description: For every task at every decision time point, the batch size cannot exceed the maximum batch size for that task when it is triggered, and must be zero when the task is not triggered.

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, for every resource at every decision time point, set the inventory carried at that point equal to the inventory carried at the previous point, adjusted by the net effect of all tasks acting on that resource, where each task contributes according to how long ago it was triggered through a fixed per-trigger effect and an effect proportional to its batch size counting only triggers that have already occurred within the task duration, and adjusted by the scheduled external inflow or outflow for that resource at that point. Second, require the inventory of every resource at every point to stay at or above its minimum allowed level. Third, require the inventory of every resource at every point to stay at or below its storage capacity. Fourth, require that whenever a task is triggered its batch size is at least the task's minimum batch size and is zero when it is not triggered. Finally, require that a task's batch size never exceeds its maximum batch size when triggered and is zero when it is not triggered.
